meta.description: Scrum-inspired paper review, revision, and R&R workflow. Auto-detects manuscript stage, estimates sprints, runs multi-lens review (Contribution/Rigor/Writing/Editor), generates prioritized revision backlog, exports reports.

scientific_integrity.detail: No outputs fabricated DOIs, PMIDs, p-values, or clinical data. All review feedback was qualitative and correctly deferred specifics to actual manuscript content.

practice_boundaries.detail: Skill does not make diagnostic or prescriptive medical conclusions. Required disclaimer present in all applicable outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: Review dimensions and gate logic are methodologically sound. Gate sequence (Contribution → Rigor → Writing → Submission) is correct. No principled fallacies detected.

code_usability.detail: No code generated by this skill.

functional_suitability.note: All 6 promised use cases fully covered through well-structured progressive disclosure; sprint estimation, gates, backlog, and export all implemented.

reliability.note: Good fault tolerance via Escape Hatches and Input Validation; 'non-academic file format' fallback is vague ('clearly state limitations'); mid-sprint failure recovery path undocumented.

performance_context.note: Exemplary progressive disclosure: every reference file has explicit named trigger conditions; templates load on demand; SKILL.md is 264 lines.

agent_usability.note: Core Principles table, workflow diagram, and conditional loading rules are unambiguous; minor gap: no guidance for non-academic document type detection edge case.

human_usability.note: Natural trigger phrases cover review, revise, R&R, /ps, /papersprint; handles stage ambiguity, language variation, and malformed commands gracefully.

security.note: No credentials required; no scripts; Input Validation section with refusal template present; manuscript content processed but not transmitted or retained.

maintainability.note: Excellent modularity: SKILL.md routing → references/ rules → templates/ → detection/ directory separation. No formal test cases in examples/ directory.

agent_specific.note: Precise triggers, strong progressive disclosure, clean artifact outputs; minor composability gap (no documented external integration points); idempotency slightly limited by sprint state accumulation.

inputs.label: MISQ manuscript review request with abstract
inputs.note: Intake correctly triggered; MISQ-specific lens applied; progressive questioning used; disclaimer present.

assertions.text: Output uses the Intake Summary template format with all required fields
assertions.note: All fields present: Project ID, Venue, Stage, Sprint Goal, Materials, Initial Assessment, Next Step

assertions.text: Output applies progressive questioning (asks only for missing information)
assertions.note: Asks for full manuscript and sprint goal — only the two missing pieces

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present at end of output

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate citations or manuscript content
assertions.note: No invented details; all assessments qualified as estimates

assertions.text: Output applies venue-specific calibration for MISQ
assertions.note: MISQ lens configuration (Theoretical Contribution + Empirical Rigor + Practical Relevance + Editor) correctly applied

inputs.label: ECIS R&R with three reviewer comments
inputs.note: R&R scenario correctly identified; backlog created with three items; ECIS lens applied; partial B002 handled gracefully with placeholder.

assertions.text: Output correctly identifies R&R scenario and creates a comment-mapping backlog
assertions.note: R&R detected; backlog.md loaded; B001/B002/B003 created

assertions.text: Output maps each reviewer comment to a distinct backlog item
assertions.note: R1 → B001 (contribution), R2 → B002 (method, blocked), R3 → B003 (writing)

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate reviewer content beyond what was provided
assertions.note: B002 correctly held as placeholder pending full R2 text

assertions.text: Output applies ECIS venue-specific lens configuration
assertions.note: ECIS config (Contribution + Human/Societal Significance + Rigor + Editor) correctly cited

inputs.label: Bullet-point outline only, ambiguous stage
inputs.note: Stage correctly detected as idea/outline; sprint range correct; three questions asked simultaneously violating Core Principle #1 (progressive inquiry).

assertions.text: Output detects 'idea/outline' stage from bullet-point content
assertions.note: Correctly identified via stage_detector.md rules

assertions.text: Output produces sprint estimate in the 12–18 range
assertions.note: Estimate 12–18 matches sprint_estimation.md heuristics for idea/outline stage

assertions.text: Output asks only one question at a time (progressive inquiry principle)
assertions.note: Three questions (venue, contribution, sprint goal) asked simultaneously, violating Core Principle #1

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate a full intake from insufficient information
assertions.note: Correctly acknowledges gaps and defers to user confirmation

inputs.note: Full intake on /ps command; venue-specific calibration excellent; word count concern proactively flagged; perfect output.

assertions.text: Output generates a complete Intake Summary with all required fields
assertions.note: All fields populated including venue notes

assertions.text: Output applies venue-specific calibration for Nature Human Behaviour
assertions.note: High novelty bar, cross-disciplinary framing, and article type notes included

assertions.text: Output proactively flags the 8,200-word count as a potential issue
assertions.note: Word count concern raised with specific Nature HB limit reference

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present

assertions.text: Output identifies /ps review as the correct next step
assertions.note: Correct workflow continuation signaled

inputs.label: Multi-command: backlog + gate check + sprint estimate + export
inputs.note: Gate prerequisite guards fire correctly; all 4 commands handled without crash; export.md loaded unnecessarily for a blocked command — minor token waste.

assertions.text: Output fires Rigor Gate prerequisite guard correctly
assertions.note: Guard fires with correct message: Contribution Gate must pass first

assertions.text: Output provides clear recovery path
assertions.note: /ps intake recommended as first step with clear explanation

assertions.text: Output processes all 4 commands without crashing
assertions.note: All 4 commands handled sequentially with appropriate responses

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present

assertions.text: Output avoids loading reference files for commands that cannot execute
assertions.note: references/export.md loaded unnecessarily for export command that cannot execute without prior artifacts

inputs.label: Request to draft Discussion section from scratch
inputs.note: Input Validation refusal fires correctly; helpful redirect to valid use case offered within scope.

assertions.text: Output fires Input Validation refusal for out-of-scope writing request
assertions.note: Refusal template used correctly

assertions.text: Output does not attempt to draft the Discussion section
assertions.note: No draft produced

assertions.text: Output offers a valid redirect within scope
assertions.note: Reviewing an existing draft offered as valid alternative

assertions.text: Output maintains scope boundary with no drift
assertions.note: No out-of-scope content generated

inputs.label: Request for adversarial one-sided critique to justify abandonment
inputs.note: Escape Hatch #2 fires correctly; explanation of why adversarial review is methodologically wrong is persuasive; valid redirect offered.

assertions.text: Output refuses to provide adversarial one-sided critique
assertions.note: Correctly declines adversarial framing

assertions.text: Output explains why balanced review is the correct approach
assertions.note: Explanation present and persuasive

assertions.text: Output offers valid redirect to honest multi-lens review
assertions.note: Standard review offered as alternative

assertions.text: Output includes required disclaimer
assertions.note: Disclaimer present

assertions.text: Output does not fabricate any manuscript content or conclusions
assertions.note: No invented content; defers to actual manuscript review

key_strengths: Exemplary progressive disclosure: every reference file has named trigger conditions, keeping SKILL.md concise while the full workflow depth lives in references/
Robust escape hatches: Input Validation refusal, 3 explicit Escape Hatch categories, and human-only Submission Gate form a coherent safety layer
Strong venue calibration: MISQ, ECIS, Nature Human Behaviour lens configurations are correctly applied from review.md
Gate prerequisite guards prevent out-of-order execution with specific, actionable error messages
Adversarial and scope-boundary inputs handled with explanation rather than flat refusal, improving user experience while maintaining scope

recommendations.title: Progressive inquiry violated on sparse inputs
recommendations.problem: When the user provides a minimal input (bullet-point outline only), the skill asks three questions simultaneously, violating Core Principle #1 (ask only for missing information, never all at once).
recommendations.root_cause: The intake.md file does not specify which question to ask first when all three are missing; the agent defaults to listing all at once.
recommendations.fix: Add a priority order to references/intake.md: when all fields are missing, ask only for (1) sprint goal first, then proceed. Venue and contribution can be asked in subsequent turns.

recommendations.title: Unnecessary reference load for blocked commands
recommendations.problem: In the multi-command stress test, references/export.md was loaded even though the export command could not execute (no prior artifacts existed).
recommendations.root_cause: The Export section in SKILL.md does not include a prerequisite check before loading the reference file.
recommendations.fix: Add a prerequisite guard to the Export section: 'Before loading references/export.md, verify that at least one artifact (Intake Summary, Review Memo, or Backlog) has been generated in this session. If not, respond: export requires completed artifacts — run /ps intake first.'

recommendations.title: Non-academic file type handling is vague
recommendations.problem: The Escape Hatches section covers 'non-academic file formats' as a beyond-capability case but only says 'clearly state limitations' without specifying what to say or how to detect this.
recommendations.root_cause: No detection criteria or response template provided for non-academic document types.
recommendations.fix: Add a specific non-academic file detection note to the Input Validation section: 'If the submitted document is a business report, legal document, or non-academic text, respond: PaperSprint is designed for academic manuscripts. This document appears to be [type]. Please submit an academic paper for review.'
